applied_optimizations.name: fused_bnorm_gelu_relu_triton_kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented fused Triton kernel for BatchNorm + GELU + ReLU. Key fixes from failed attempt: (1) Used tl.math.erf for exact GELU instead of tanh approximation, (2) Added explicit .to(tl.float32) casts for all loads/stores, (3) Used BLOCK_SIZE as multiple of 64 for CDNA4 wavefront alignment, (4) Computed batch mean/variance in training mode to match PyTorch BatchNorm behavior.

attempted_but_failed.name: tl.libdevice.tanh for GELU
attempted_but_failed.reason: tl.libdevice is unavailable on ROCm Triton

attempted_but_failed.name: tanh approximation for GELU
attempted_but_failed.reason: GELU tanh approximation has ~0.00047 max error vs exact GELU, exceeding fp32 tolerance of 0.0001

attempted_but_failed.name: using running_mean/running_var in training mode
attempted_but_failed.reason: BatchNorm in training mode computes batch statistics, not running statistics

notes: ROCm Triton does not have tl.libdevice - use tl.math.erf for exact GELU
BatchNorm training mode computes per-batch mean/variance, must match this in kernel
BLOCK_SIZE should be multiple of 64 for CDNA4 wavefront alignment
Achieved 1.49x speedup (5.83ms -> 3.91ms) with correct fused kernel

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output, same as trial 3. Two consecutive crashes suggest the agent is attempting changes that break the kernel without verifying it first. Need to establish a working baseline befo

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Three consecutive trials (3, 4, 5) produced no output, indicating the agent keeps making changes that crash. Need to enforce a verify-first workflow and suggest a safer optimization approach.
